business.headline: Govt hikes special duty on diesel, aviation fuel exports amid West Asia crisisThe Centre has increased the windfall profit tax on diesel exports to Rs 14 per litre and on ATF exports to Rs 12.5 per litre from June 16, while retaining the petrol export duty at Rs 1.5 per litre.

India-US trade deal enters final lap as fresh talks scheduled on June 23-24India and the United States will hold trade talks on June 23 and 24 to finalise the first tranche of their proposed bilateral trade agreement. The meeting could shape the pace of the wider pact as both sides work through market access and trade barriers.

The hidden intelligence gap in India's renewable energy transitionA utility-scale solar-plus-storage project in India was found to be losing Rs 20–25 lakh a month despite strong uptime. The case highlights why India's renewable build-out needs software-led grid and market optimisation.

Sensex ends 736 points higher, Nifty above 23,800; IndiGo up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex surged 736.38 points, or 0.97%, to settle at 76,264.33, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 231 points, or 0.98%, to close at 23,853.90. The rally was broad-based, with strong buying seen in auto, real estate, industrial and consumption-related stocks.

Why the US-Iran peace deal is good news for IndiaThe reported US-Iran agreement to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz has eased immediate pressure on India's oil-dependent economy. Its broader benefit will depend on whether lower crude prices and calmer trade routes hold.

Strait of Hormuz opens again, but will petrol and diesel prices fall?The Strait of Hormuz has reopened after a US-Iran understanding eased immediate supply concerns. The move may cool crude prices, but any relief in petrol, diesel and LPG is likely to be limited and gradual.

E20 fuel and car insurance: Will your claim be affected?ICICI Lombard has flagged that E20-linked damage claims in older vehicles may not be straightforward. The issue has sharpened concerns over whether fuel-related wear, negligence or policy exclusions could leave owners paying repair costs.

Why crude oil, monsoon and fertiliser prices are worrying the governmentNirmala Sitharaman said India faces risks from crude oil, foreign exchange, fertiliser prices and weaker rainfall. She said the government has kept buffer stocks ready as import and supply pressures persist.

Sensex jumps 1,200 points over US-Iran peace deal: Is worst over for investors?Markets surged after the US and Iran signalled a deal to end hostilities and restore Strait of Hormuz shipping. The rebound tracked falling crude prices, though analysts said investors should remain cautious.

Sensex jumps 1,200 points: Why is stock market rising today?The biggest trigger behind today's rally was a potential peace agreement between the United States and Iran. The two countries indicated that an initial deal had been reached to end the conflict and resume shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

Sensex surges 1,000 points as investors cheer Iran-US peace dealThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,097.05 points, or 1.45%, to 76,625.00 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 333.90 points, or 1.41%, to 23,955.95 as of 9:25 am.

Why your salary is losing the race against home pricesIndia's housing dream is facing its biggest affordability test as rising prices force buyers to choose between bigger debts, longer commutes and delayed dreams.

FDs, SCSS or mutual funds? The best way to invest Rs 50 lakh after retirementRetiring with Rs 50 lakh may sound reassuring, but the real challenge begins after retirement: how do you turn that lump sum into a steady monthly income without running out of money?

US Justice Department okays Paramount's $110 billion Warner Bros takeoverAfter an eight-month review, the DOJ concluded the proposed merger would strengthen competition across streaming, television and film, potentially benefiting consumers, workers and the broader entertainment industry.

SpaceX opens at $150 in strong Nasdaq debut, up 11% from IPO priceSpaceX surged more than 20 per cent in its Nasdaq debut, lifting its valuation above USD 2 trillion as investors piled into the world's biggest IPO. The stock opened at USD 150, signalling strong demand and easing concerns over its market debut.

Groceries, fuel, school fees: Is your monthly budget about to get tighter?School fees, petrol, vegetables, utility bills—the list of monthly expenses never seems to end. As inflation rises, many families are asking the same question: is their budget about to get even tighter?

Inflation rises to 3.93% for May as West Asia conflict pushes up fuel and food pricesFood inflation continued to move higher in May. It stood at 4.78%, up from 4.20% in April, showing that prices of food items are rising faster than they were a month earlier.

Markets rally on easing West Asia tensions, oil falling below $88The S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,695.40 points, or 2.30%, to close at 75,527.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 461.30 points, or 1.99%, to settle at 23,622.90.

Salary mismatch to missed interest: 7 ITR mistakes that can trigger tax noticesFiling your ITR may seem straightforward, but even a minor reporting error can attract the attention of the tax department. Here are seven mistakes you should watch out for.

The world's biggest IPO starts trading today. Is Elon Musk's SpaceX overvalued?SpaceX begins trading on Nasdaq after a record $75 billion IPO and a $1.75 trillion valuation. The debut has sharpened questions over valuation, Musk's control, xAI losses and broader market liquidity.

entertainment.headline: Doctor on the Edge review: Lee Jae-wook, Shin Ye-eun make familiar formula workRelease Date:Mon Jun 01, 2026 Doctor on the Edge follows plastic surgeon Do Ji-ui as he lands on a remote island and clashes with nurse Yook Ha-ri. The series uses medical emergencies and small-town life to explore loneliness, burnout and trust.

Zendaya-Tom's rare outing for Spider-Man: Brand New Day event breaks the internet Zendaya and Tom Holland stepped out together in Madrid to launch Spider-Man: Brand New Day. The appearance renewed attention on both the film and ongoing marriage speculation around the couple.

Kala Hiran producer sends legal notice to actor Govind Namdev, demands apology Producer Amit Jani has sent a legal notice to actor Govind Namdev over his remarks on Kala Hiran. The dispute has widened the controversy around the film as Salman Khan also seeks a stay in court.

Sanchita Ugale death: Roadies 8 winner Aanchal alleges exploitation, industry pressure Roadies 8 winner and actor Aanchal Khurana reacted to TV actor Sanchita Ugale's death and spoke about the pressure actors face. Her remarks pointed to alleged exploitation, rejection and the mental strain she says the industry often ignores.

Only he can: Jr NTR praises 94-year-old Singeetham Srinivasa Rao's new film attempt Actor Jr NTR praised the recent film Sing Geetham, calling the filmmaker Singeetham Srinivasa Rao's creativity and wisdom at 94 deeply inspiring. The veteran director made his directorial comeback with the latest movie.

Munawar Faruqui says stop trolling Pranit More: There has to be a limit Actor-comedian Munawar Faruqui urged people to stop trolling Pranit More and others over their controversial remarks during a comedy show. He said they have already faced serious consequences and asked people to let the legal process take its course.

Rashmika signs off as 'Mandanna Deverakonda' under Vijay's post, fans react Rashmika Mandanna referred to herself as Rashmika Mandanna Deverakonda at a Telangana scholarship event and in an X reply to Vijay Deverakonda. The exchange drew attention alongside The Deverakonda Foundation's initiative for 180 government school students.

Actor Anaswara Rajan joins fans at Allu Arjun's Happy re-release, dances to songs Actor Anaswara Rajan joined her sister and friends for a screening of Allu Arjun's Happy in Kerala. The 4K re-release has triggered nostalgic celebrations as fans sing and dance to its songs.

Peddi extended version includes Janhvi scenes from June 17, not controversial bits The makers of Peddi have announced an extended cut from June 17, adding back three deleted scenes, including two featuring Janhvi Kapoor's Achiyyamma, after backlash over her portrayal.

25 years of Gadar: Sunny Deol thanks fans for keeping Tara Singh alive Actor Sunny Deol celebrated 25 years of Gadar: Ek Prem Katha with a heartfelt note. He thanked fans for keeping Tara Singh and the film alive in their hearts.

Isakhapatnam trailer: Samuthirakani, Aishwarya Rajesh clash for power in crime saga Prime Video has unveiled the trailer for its Telugu Original series Isakapatnam ahead of its July 2 release. The seven-episode crime thriller centres on power, revenge and rivalries in a fictional port town.

I am a man, and I am disgusted by Pranit More-Madhur Virli's 'jokes' As a man, I find Pranit More and Madhur Virli's jokes deeply humiliating. They offend me, yes, but they normalise the violation of women and make every decent man carry the burden of rebuilding trust.

Don't like item songs? Peddi lyricist says you are the 'item' As the debate over Peddi's dance number, Hellello, continues to grow online, lyricist Anantha Sriram has now responded to the criticism. Speaking at the film's success event, he defended the song and the makers' creative choices.

25 years on, Gadar director says Sunny's iconic hand-pump scene was called illogical At the film's 25-year milestone, director Anil Sharma said Gadar's hand-pump scene almost stalled because many in the team found it illogical. He said Sunny Deol backed the idea, and the moment went on to become a cultural landmark.

Who is Dishani Chakraborty? Mithun's adopted daughter engaged to Myles Mantzaris Dishani Chakraborty announced her engagement to Myles Mantzaris with intimate proposal pictures. The post has renewed curiosity about Mithun Chakraborty's adopted daughter and her Hollywood career.

Mithun Chakraborty's daughter Dishani engaged to colourist Myles Mantzaris Dishani Chakraborty, daughter of Mithun Chakraborty and Yogeeta Bali, is engaged to longtime partner Myles Mantzaris. The couple shared glimpses of their seaside proposal on Instagram and revealed that they will get married on December 6, 2026.

Top IMDb-rated movies and shows recently added to OTT platforms From Dhurandhar: The Revenge and Made in India: A Titan Story to Sapne vs Everyone and The Boys, several top IMDb-rated movies and shows have recently arrived on OTT platforms. Here's a look at the highest-rated titles worth streaming this week.

Super Subbu: Sundeep Kishan turns sex ed teacher in Netflix's July release Netflix has announced Super Subbu, its first Telugu original series starring Sundeep Kishan, Mithila Palkar and Murali Sharma. The comedy-drama will premiere in July.

Gadar cast then and now: Where are they 25 years later? Gadar: Ek Prem Katha has completed 25 years since its release. Here's what Sunny Deol, Ameesha Patel and the rest of the film's memorable cast are doing today.

general news.headline: The $300-billion weak link in US-Iran peace deal that could restart the warAmid the US-Iran peace deal, here is a look at how Iran has seemingly turned postwar negotiations into economic leverage. There is perhaps a reason why President Trump is eager to project the deal as a victory, and how the proposed $300-billion reconstruction package might leave the peace agreement in tatters.

Trump, Vance, Iran Speaker sign MoU to end 4-month war, formal ceremony on FridayThe memorandum lays the foundation for a peace agreement between Washington and Tehran, although officials indicated that several key issues still require further negotiations.

Sooryavanshi pushes Sri Lanka A player, exchanges words after India A lossVaibhav Sooryavanshi was involved in a heated argument with a Sri Lanka A fielder and pushed him after India A's super over loss in Dambulla on Monday, June 15.

FIFA World Cup Live: (BEL 1-1 EGY), Points shared in Seattle after thrillerBelgium vs Egypt Live Score, FIFA World Cup 2026: Belgium and Egypt share the spoils in Seattle after a thrilling contest. Egypt took the lead through Ashour before Belgium got the equaliser in the 66th minute. A grand finish followed as both teams had chances to win all three points.

Stanford students walk out during Sundar Pichai's graduation speechStanford University's graduation ceremony saw a group of students walk out during Google CEO Sundar Pichai's keynote address, protesting over the company's reported contracts with government and defence-linked agencies. The event also saw Pichai keep his speech focused on general advice for graduates, avoiding deeper discussion on technology topics.
US Air Force B-52 bomber crashes after takeoff at Edwards Air Force BaseUS Air Force B-52 Stratofortress crashed shortly after takeoff from Edwards Air Force Base in California

After Iran breakthrough, Trump says he can help end Russia-Ukraine conflictWith the Iran agreement now moving towards formal implementation, Trump indicated that ending the war in Ukraine would be his next major objective.

US-Iran Deal Live: Netanyahu says Iran will never get nuclear weapons, deal or no dealUS-Israel-Iran Live Updates: The United States and Iran took a major step towards ending their months-long conflict. US President Donald Trump announced that a memorandum of understanding between the two countries had already been signed ahead of a formal ceremony in Geneva on Friday. The framework agreement is expected to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and begin a 60-day negotiation process aimed at resolving outstanding disputes, including Iran's nuclear programme. Trump said shipping traffic had already started returning to the strait, helping ease concerns in global energy markets. However, the full text of the agreement remains unpublished, with further details expected in the coming days. Despite the diplomatic breakthrough, tensions persist across the region. Lebanon has emerged as a key point of contention, with Iran insisting the agreement requires an end to hostilities there, while Israel says it will continue acting against Hezbollah if necessary. Fresh Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon shortly after the deal was announced. In his first comments since the agreement became public, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu reaffirmed that Israel would prevent Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons regardless of any deal and said the country's security campaign was far from over. He also vowed that Israeli forces would remain in security zones where they see ongoing threats. To catch all the live updates, follow our live blog on indiatoday.in.
Why the Brazil rope jump is a death trap, even with a rope attachedNot attaching the rope was not the only issue at the popular rope-jumping spot in Brazil where a 21-year-old woman lost her life.

MBA era is over in India: What Chief Economic Adviser says about education's futureChief Economic Adviser V Anantha Nageswaran has urged young Indians to look beyond degrees and build practical, hard-to-automate skills. He said employability and economic growth will depend equally on vocational ability and human judgement.

Govt hikes special duty on diesel, aviation fuel exports amid West Asia crisisThe Centre has increased the windfall profit tax on diesel exports to Rs 14 per litre and on ATF exports to Rs 12.5 per litre from June 16, while retaining the petrol export duty at Rs 1.5 per litre.

Dhaka, Delhi in diplomatic row after Bangladesh PM's aide stopped at IGI AirportBangladesh Prime Minister Tarique Rahman's adviser, Zahed Ur Rahman, was stopped at New Delhi's IGI Airport for over two hours before being cleared to enter India. However, the BNP leader's aide chose to return to Dhaka. Bangladesh has now summoned the top Indian diplomat to convey its 'deep disappointment' over the incident.

Video: IndiGo pilot's tribute to farmers who boarded Noida airport's first flightNearly 170 villagers from the Jewar region, whose land was acquired for the development of Noida International Airport, boarded the first passenger flights from the newly operational airport on Monday.

27-year-old Telangana techie found dead in farmhouse pool, police suspect foul playA Hyderabad software employee was found dead in a farmhouse pool in Yadadri Bhuvanagiri after a company get-together. Police are probing injuries on his body as they examine accidental drowning and foul play.

Netanyahu backed Trump's war. Now Israel is having hard time living with his peaceThe war against Iran brought Trump and Netanyahu together. The peace deal may pull them apart. Read on to find out why.

I am a man, and I am disgusted by Pranit More-Madhur Virli's 'jokes'As a man, I find Pranit More and Madhur Virli's jokes deeply humiliating. They offend me, yes, but they normalise the violation of women and make every decent man carry the burden of rebuilding trust.

Cadaver code: The dead is the teacher here. A nation needs to take lessonsGiving our body for medical studies, knowing it will be dissected multiple times, is the highest form of donation. This is how cadavers are received, preserved, used with respect and disposed of. But India needs to urgently improve the process of final send-off for cadavers, which might help end their scarcity too.

UPSC civil services prelims 2026 results declared, 13,343 candidates qualify for MainsUPSC has shortlisted 13,343 candidates for the Civil Services Mains 2026. Qualified aspirants must complete mandatory registration and detail verification between June 19 and June 28 to appear for the next stage.

Monsoon on casual stroll, parts of India to witness mercury rise on TuesdayThe IMD has forecast advancing monsoon rains, thunderstorms and heatwave pockets across India on Tuesday, but it will be slow.

Priyank Kharge seeks RSS funding, legal status details; Mohan Bhagwat respondsKarnataka Home Minister Priyank Kharge asked RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat to disclose the organisation's legal status, funding, expenditure and permissions. Bhagwat rejected the demand as political gimmick.

Lost the game, and grace: Tilak and Sooryavanshi lead ugly Super Over meltdownTalent Tri-Series: A chaotic tie in Dambulla ended in an ugly Super Over defeat to Sri Lanka A on Monday. Shocking tactical blunders by India A were made worse by Tilak Varma and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi losing temper.

Online gaming, family trips: Cashier blows Rs 41 lakh in bank funds, loses Rs 35 lakhPolice have arrested a Khargone cooperative bank cashier accused of stealing over Rs 41 lakh. Investigators are checking her claim that most of the money was lost in online gaming and tracing whether others were involved.

How Kundu couple could turn nondescript NCPI into 5th-biggest party in Lok SabhaThe Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI) has never won a single seat, contested just one election in which it secured a total of 822 votes, and has a funding of Rs 1.13 lakh. Yet, the Trinamool Congress (TMC) rebellion might help it become the fifth-largest party in the Lok Sabha and the second-largest in the BJP-led NDA. Here's more about the curious case of the NCPI and its Kundu couple.

TMC rebel MP seeks Kalyan Banerjee's expulsion from Parliament over 'misconduct'The latest development comes a day after Dastidar and other rebel TMC MPs joined the little-known Tripura-based Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI) and decided to back the BJP-led National Democratic Alliance (NDA) at the Centre.

sports.headline: Cape Verde's Vozinha says visa problems kept his mother from witnessing history FIFA World Cup 2026: Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha said US visa problems stopped his mother and grandparents from attending his World Cup debut. His account has added a personal note to wider concerns over entry barriers around the tournament.

How a LinkedIn message made Roberto Lopes one of Cape Verde's World Cup heroes Roberto Lopes anchored Cape Verde's defence in a goalless World Cup draw against Spain. However, this moment almost didn't happen for the 33-year-old as he almost ignored a LinkedIn message from Cape Verde FA to play for the team.

Who is Vozinha? Cape Verde's ageless hero who kept Spain at bay for a famous point FIFA World Cup 2026: Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha waited until age 40 for his World Cup debut and seized the moment, producing seven saves against Spain to earn a famous point and create history.

Heroic Cape Verde mark World Cup debut by stealing point off lacklustre Spain Cape Verde held Spain to a draw on their World Cup debut as Vozinha resisted a late surge. The result exposed Spain's lack of firepower in attack without the presence of Lamine Yamal, who failed to get the win for his side.

Shreyanka Patil recalls dark injury phase: Was depressed, wanted to quit cricket Women's T20 World Cup: Shreyanka Patil said she was depressed and wanted to quit cricket during the phase, where she suffered a finger injury followed by Grade 3 shin splints in both legs.

Sourav Ganguly lodges police complaint over Facebook page damaging his reputation Former India captain Sourav Ganguly has filed a police complaint against a Facebook page and media platform over a fake post falsely claiming an ED raid at his residence, alleging it was defamatory and damaging to his reputation.

FIFA World Cup Live: (BEL 1-1 EGY), Lukaku drags Belgium back in Seattle Belgium vs Egypt Live Score, FIFA World Cup 2026: Romelu Lukaku makes an instant impact to bring Belgium back level against Egypt and set up a grand finish in Seattle.

Tunisian turmoil: Hours after Sweden thrashing, African team sack coach Tunisia reportedly sacked head coach Sabri Lamouchi after a 5-1 World Cup opening defeat to Sweden. Lamouchi took charge of the team in January this year.

Lost the game, and grace: Tilak and Sooryavanshi lead ugly Super Over meltdown Talent Tri-Series: A chaotic tie in Dambulla ended in an ugly Super Over defeat to Sri Lanka A on Monday. Shocking tactical blunders by India A were made worse by Tilak Varma and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi losing temper.

England name Ben Stokes-less XI for 2nd Test, Jofra Archer returns ENG vs NZ, 2nd Test: England have named a revamped playing XI for the second Test against New Zealand, with Joe Root continuing as captain in the absence of Ben Stokes, who remains unavailable amid an investigation into a nightclub controversy.

Sooryavanshi pushes Sri Lanka A player, exchanges words after India A loss Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was involved in a heated argument with a Sri Lanka A fielder and pushed him after India A's super over loss in Dambulla on Monday, June 15.

Real Madrid sign Marc Cucurella from Chelsea on six-year dealReal Madrid have signed Chelsea left-back Marc Cucurella on a six-year contract. The move underlines Madrid's rebuild under Jose Mourinho and deepens Chelsea's summer reshuffle.

Real Madrid make rare signing of ex-Barcelona, La Masia playerReal Madrid have signed Marc Cucurella from Chelsea on a six-year contract. The move stands out because the Spain left-back was developed at Barcelona's La Masia academy.

Neeraj Chopra set for return after being sidelined by injury for almost a yearJavelin star Neeraj Chopra is set for a return after spending nearly a year on the sidelines. Neeraj will make his return in the Doha Diamond League, set to be hosted on June 19.

F1: Lewis Hamilton gets a heart from Kim Kardashian after winning Barcelona GPBarcelona-Catalunya GP: Lewis Hamilton's first Ferrari win has drawn an immediate public reaction from Kim Kardashian. Her celebratory post, and Hamilton's own comments, have pushed relationship rumours back into the spotlight.

After setting the IPL alight, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi searches for second gearIndia A vs Sri Lanka A, Talent tri-series: 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is yet to go past 50 in the ongoing series in Dambulla. on Monday, he was dismissed for 21 off 14 balls against Sri Lanka A.

Cyril Gane halts Alex Pereira's bid at history in White House specialUFC: Alex Pereira historic bid for three UFC titles in three separate divisions was halted by Frenchman Ciryl Gane, who knocked out his opponent in the second round of the showpiece fight of the night.

VAR official's 'OK hand' gesture during FIFA World Cup broadcast sparks outrageFIFA World Cup 2026: Australian VAR official Shaun Evans was accused online of flashing a reverse OK sign before Germany vs Curacao. The image now tests FIFA's anti-discrimination message as campaigners demand his removal.

Brainfade? Vipraj Nigam penalised twice for running on pitch, India A docked 10 runsVipraj Nigam was penalised twice for running on the protected area during India A's innings against Sri Lanka A in Dambulla. The repeated breach, after an earlier warning to Anukul Roy, cost India A 10 runs.

FIFA using cricket's tech in World Cup: Snicko saves the day in Sweden vs TunisiaFIFA World Cup 2026: A cricket-style "Snicko" system was used in VAR review to determine whether Alexander Isak had made a faint touch in the build-up to Sweden's fourth goal against Tunisia.

From Chambal to India U19 captain: Yashbardhan Singh Chauhan's journey in blueA 17-year-old from Gwalior has been named India's U-19 captain for the Sri Lanka tour. Behind the appointment lies a father's dream, a mother's prayers, and a principal who saw a leader long before the selectors did.

Goal fest in Monterrey as Sweden put five past TunisiaYasin Ayari scored twice as Sweden beat Tunisia 5-1 in Monterrey, opening with a stunning early strike he chose not to celebrate. Sweden's control was reinforced by Tunisia's errors and a technology-backed review that allowed Svanberg's record-fast substitute goal to stand.

IND A vs SL A Highlights: Sooryavanshi in big fight with Sri Lanka after India lose Super OverIndia A (IND) vs Sri Lanka A (SL) Highlights, Tri-Series 2026: Sri Lanka have beaten India in the Super Over in Dambulla. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi came out to bat in the Super Over, but it wasn't enough for India to win.

Why didn't Sweden's Yasin Ayari celebrate after scoring against Tunisia?FIFA World Cup 2026: Yasin Ayari fired Sweden ahead against Tunisia with a magnificent early strike and chose not to celebrate. His restraint, shaped by family ties, gave a sharp human edge to a frantic Group F first half.

Somali referee Omar Artan, denied entry to USA, set to be paid in full by FIFAFIFA is set to pay Omar Artan in full despite him not officiating in the FIFA World Cup 2026. The Somalian referee was denied entry to the USA and was sent back from the airport.

Amad Diallo late dagger helps Ivory Coast to 1-0 win over EcuadorFIFA World Cup 2026: Philadelphia was painted in Ecuador's colours and La Tri created enough chances to win. But a late moment of brilliance from Amad Diallo handed Ivory Coast a dramatic 1-0 victory and an early advantage in Group E.

Japan fans win hearts again by cleaning Dallas stadium after Dutch thrillerFIFA World Cup 2026: Japan supporters cleaned the Dallas Stadium stands after their 2-2 World Cup draw with the Netherlands. The gesture went viral again and highlighted a long-running culture of the Blue Samurai fans.

Lamine Yamal likely off bench for Spain's World Cup opener against Cape VerdeFIFA World Cup 2026: Spain have received a fitness boost ahead of their World Cup opener, but fans may have to wait a little longer to see Lamine Yamal from the start. Luis de la Fuente confirmed the teenager is fit but not ready for a full match.

Iran land in US for FIFA World Cup opener after landmark peace dealIran's World Cup campaign has unfolded amid extraordinary political circumstances. Team Melli arrived in Los Angeles ahead of their opener against New Zealand just hours before a US-Iran peace deal was announced.

What's in that notebook? Fans think Japan coach has a Death NoteJapan coach Hajime Moriyasu's tactical changes against the Netherlands reignited the famous Death Note meme, leaving many fans wondering about the story behind the joke.

SWE 5-1 TUN, World Cup Highlights: Sweden beat Tunisia with some comfort at Stadio MonterreySweden (SWE) vs Tunisia (TUN) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Sweden cruised to a comfortable win over Tunisia as they scored five at the Stadio Monterrey, thanks to Isak, Gyokeres, Ayari and Svanberg. Tunisia got one back through Rekik before half-time but Sweden looked comfortable for the victory.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
